item.title: Fender Player II Jazz Bass – Black/Maple – Lollar Pickups, Emerson Wiring, Plek’d — $1,100
item.description:
$1,100 · Very Good · Raul's Gear Garage

Fender Player II Jazz Bass in Black with maple fingerboard. Upgraded with Lollar 60s Jazz Bass pickups and an Emerson Custom prewired wiring harness. The bass has also been professionally Plek’d. In excellent playing condition and functions exactly as it should. Amazing sounding bass guitar.

item.title: ZON Sonus 5/2 Carbon Graphite Neck 5-String Bass - (35" Scale) — $2,990
item.description:
$2,990 · Very Good · Theodore's Gear Outlet

  • Sonus 5/2 configuration: 5-string with two Bartolini pickups (neck: wide-aperture split-coil / P-bass style soapbar; bridge: wide-aperture dual-coil/humbucker). These are custom-wound for Zon.
  • Five string 35” inch scale.
  • Alder body:
  • Gold chrome hardware/pickups cover + gold paint/finish:
  • Built: May 1999 "Original owner"
  • Custom thumb rest and dual strap system.

These are premium USA-made basses with graphite necks, excellent build quality, and a modern hi-fi tone with great clarity and punch from the Bartolini's.


My Experience with the Gold Zon:

Carbon graphite neck, that I never had to mess with, doesn’t matter the temperature the humidity or stand type, it’s simply stays straight. Lowest action I’ve ever seen on a bass. 40-125 or 45-130 strings, worked best. Plays like a Formula One race car. When you slap pluck a string, it’s snappy because the pluck goes right to the string and not absorbed into the neck flex.

Versatile tone that works with nearly any style, clean massive to tight growly tone. Natural sounding, large soap bar Bartolini pickups.

-Volume Control

-Pick up blend, front to back

-Treble pot stacked on top of Bass pot

I’ve played several top end bass guitars and nothing plays like this Zon! How the fret board merges into the body makes slapping so nice on the thumb. 35" scale delivers a nice low B!

*Comes with soft shell case not hard case

See photos for condition. There is spots on the back and the bottom of the bass where non denting dings took the gold paint off, revealing the under black primer layer. Could be matched and restored.

item.title: 🎸Acoustic Electric Bass Guitar with a Rosewood Fretboard +Soft Case/Gig Bag +Strap +Cable +Picks!🎸 — $175
item.description:
$175 · Brand New · Leo Blacks Music Shack

****I accept 100% of ALL refund requests plus return shipping!****

Hi! I’m Leo Black, owner of “Leo Black’s Music Shack” and whether you're playing alone, with friends, family, at church, or on stage, you'll always sound AND look good with this beauty!

She’s a jet black acoustic-electric (Or acoustoelectric, depending on your background) bass guitar and yes, she sounds AND plays as good as she looks! I bought one for myself last year expecting a big toy that would last a few months if I was lucky, but I'm still playing her! She's amazing! The action was perfect right out of the box, the electric pickup sends a crystal clear signal to any amp or PA, and the fretboard is the same material used on Made in America Fenders! O.O

Yeah. She's awesome. And she's also available in natural or blue. Just ask for pictures! :)

My story: I've been selling guitars and guitar accessories to support local musicians with my live music production company (ICVMproductions) for over 6 years until COVID shut us down. Now, in an effort to earn the funds needed to keep the music alive, we’re opening our on-line store, “Leo Black’s Music Shack & Guitar Emporium.”

Thanks for checking out my ad, and, If you have any questions, please don’t hesitate to ask! :)

~ Leo

****I accept 100% of ALL refund requests plus return shipping!****

For more colors and styles check out our website!

item.description:

This is a fantastic bass! I'm only playing 4-strings these days, but when I played 5-strings, this was my #1. Super fast neck, perfect balance, snarling tone, string through body, powerful 2-band EQ, and a killer appearance. Nails that vintage pre-Ernie Ball vibe and tone. Free USA shipping. item.title: Warrick FNA Corvette 1998 - Amber — $1,675
item.description:
$1,675 · Excellent · The Hebrew Hammer's Groove Studio

Selling a spectacular, player-ready 1998 Warwick FNA 4-String bass guitar. This is a golden-era, German-built instrument from the late 90s, boasting premium tonewoods and features that modern models no longer offer.

Top:

Stunning carved AAA Flame Maple top with beautiful, deep figure separated by a thin strip of walnut

Body:

Lightweight, resonant swamp ash back.

Neck:

Solid wenge neck with a wenge fingerboard—highly sought after for its incredible stability and legendary Warwick "growl."

Electronics:

Single Music Man-style Seymour Duncan Basslines humbucker paired with an active Seymour Duncan 3-band preamp. Features a push/pull volume pot for a mid-scooped "slap" mode.

Condition:

Excellent structural and playing condition. Normal, light cosmetic players' wear for its age, but completely unmolested.

Included is the original, heavily padded Warwick RockBag.

This bass punches through any live or studio mix with aggressive, percussive mids and tight lows. It plays beautifully with low action

item.title: Ken Smith Proto-J Bass - Natural — $585
item.description:
$585 · Very Good · Milwaukee Vintage

Serial #: 03101150

Weight: 8lb, 12.7oz

This Ken Smith Proto-J Bass features an ash body with a maple neck and fretboard with block inlays. It is equipped with a pair of J-style single coils that have a 2-band active bass/treble EQ, pickup blend, and master volume controls. It plays great with low action and the frets are in nice shape with minimal wear. This bass is in very good shape overall with light scratches throughout the top, pickguard, and control plate. Comes with an unoriginal gig bag.

item.title: Squier Affinity Series Active Jazz Bass, Laurel FB, Mystic Sea Foam Green — $499.99
item.description:
$499.99 · Brand New · Gelb Music's Reverb Store

Squier Affinity Series Active Jazz Bass, Laurel Fingerboard, White Pickguard, Mystic Sea Foam Green

Description

A superb gateway into the time-honored Fender® family, the Squier® Affinity Series® Active Jazz Bass® delivers legendary design and quintessential tone for today’s aspiring bassist. This J Bass® features several player-friendly refinements such as a thin and lightweight body, a slim and comfortable “C”-shaped neck with retro block inlays and vintage-style open-gear tuning machines for smooth, accurate tuning. Loaded with two Squier Noiseless™ J Bass pickups shaped by an active 9V 2-band preamp for a wide variety of punchy tones, this model is ready to help lay the foundation for any player at any stage.

Features

  • Thin and lightweight body
  • Slim “C”-shaped neck with block inlays
  • Squier Noiseless pickups
  • Active 9-volt preamp with controls for volume, blend, and bass/treble boost/cut
  • 4-saddle bass bridge

Product Specs:

General

  • Series: Affinity Series®
  • Orientation: Right-Hand
  • Color: Mystic Sea Foam Green
  • Country Of Origin: China
  • Platform: Squier Basses

Neck

  • Neck Material: Maple
  • Neck Finish: Satin Urethane with Gloss Urethane Headstock Face
  • Neck Shape: "C" Shape
  • Neck Construction: Bolt-On
  • Fingerboard Radius: 9.5" (241 mm)
  • Fingerboard Material: Indian Laurel
  • Position Inlays: Pearloid Block
  • Side Dots: White
  • Number of Frets: 20
  • Truss Rod: Single Action, Head Adjust
  • String Nut: Synthetic Bone
  • Nut Width: 1.5" (38.1 mm)
  • Nut Material: Synthetic Bone
  • Fret Size: Medium Jumbo
  • Scale Length: 34" (864 mm)

Body

  • Body: Poplar
  • Body Finish: Gloss Polyurethane
  • Body Shape: Jazz Bass®
  • Body Material: Poplar

Hardware

  • Bridge: 4-Saddle Standard
  • Pickguard: 3-Ply White
  • Pickup Covers: Black
  • Control Knobs: Black Plastic
  • Hardware Finish: Chrome
  • Tuning Machines: Vintage-Style
  • String Trees: Vintage-Style Disc
  • Strings: Nickel Plated Steel (.045-.105 Gauges)
  • Neck Plate: 4-Bolt Squier®
  • Strap Buttons: Standard

Electronics

  • Bridge Pickup: Squier® Ceramic Noiseless™ Jazz Bass®
  • Neck Pickup: Squier® Ceramic Noiseless™ Jazz Bass®
  • Pickup Configuration: SS
  • Controls: Master Volume, Blend Knob, Active Bass/Treble Boost/Cut (Stacked Control)
  • Switching: None

item.title: Steinberger L2. All this machinery making modern music? Black 1983 — $6,295
item.description:
$6,295 · Excellent · GrinningElk Music Co.

Just rummaging around Facebook the other day (you know, looking for potential deals), when nestled between "Getting herpes was a gift" and "Toxicity and gym culture," there it was... the treasure of the Sierra Madre. Finding gold in my business looks a lot like what you see, without penicillin.

Without hesitation, a call was placed, and a locale was chosen. You know the old routine: "I'll meet you at such-and-such, and I'll be driving the..." Why? Because urgency and despair don't get along well. No gear to sell creates urgency, and stomach rumblings equate to despair, and nobody likes despair, said the Joker to the Thief. What do we love, people? That's it—prosperity and a really nice Steinberger.

That led me to this early-production Steinberger L2.

The first thing I noticed when he placed it in my hands was the condition. Forty-plus years later, it still looks like it just landed off a spaceship. Carbon graphite construction. No headstock. Perfect balance. Ridiculous sustain. A bass that rewrote the rules long before the rest of the industry figured out what the question was.

This example remains original with the exception of a newer Steinberger flip-out leg rest. The condition is exceptional, the fretwear is minimal, and the unmistakable Steinberger tone is exactly what you'd expect from one of the early Newburgh instruments. Roll back the tone and you'll get that signature Steinberger growl. Open it up, and you'll hear why players like Geddy Lee made these famous.

In closing, what did we discuss? Rummaging through Facebook, gym culture, herpes, prosperity... and, of course, this Steinberger bass.

How's that, Geddy?

"All this machinery making modern music..."

And now you can see where he got his inspiration. Well, if he had a functional crystal ball that is.

item.title: 1991 Rickenbacker 4003 Bass w/ Bartolini TCT Preamp, Fireglo, Made in USA, with case — $2,899
item.description:
$2,899 · Very Good · Coast Sonic

Maker: Rickenbacker

Model: 4003

Condition: very good

Description: 

***Please note Reverb only allows for 25 photos per listing. More photos available on our website.***

***Please contact us for international shipping quote***

Here for your consideration is a 1991 Rickenbacker 4003 Bass in very good condition! This one features a Bartonlini Active Preamp/eq giving this 4003 a wide variety of tones on tap. The control cavity has been professionally routed to fit the preamp and the second "Rick-O-Sound" jack has been disconnected. Body shows some general playwear: Finish marks, scratches, dings, buckle rash, finish wear, ect., but is overall in great shape for its age and shows no major damage or issues. Rear of neck shows some surface marks and a couple of very small dings but overall is in great shape with no major damage or blemishes. Headstock shows some surface marks, dings, and what appears to be some finish reaction from hanging on a wall hanger (see photos for details), but is otherwise in great shape. Fretboard is in excellent condition and frets show only minimal wear. Binding shows cracks along the fretboard, and in a few spots along the body. See photos for details. Hardware shows some light age/wear but is in good shape overall. Electronics have been thoroughly inspected and cleaned and work perfectly. Features original pickups and a Bartolini Active preamp/EQ that adds a whole new dimension to this 4003. Could very easily be returned to stock wiring as well if the preamp isn't your thing. Overall This Rick plays amazing, has just the right amount of wear and character, and with the added Bartolini preamp can go well beyond the range of available tones of a run of the mill 4003. Has been fully inspected and set up by our tech and ready for a new home. Includes original hard case with some original documents and bridge pickup cover.. 

Weight: 9lbs 4oz

Neck Profile: C

Neck Depth: 1st fret .862", 12th fret .895"

Fretboard Radius: 10"

Nut Width: 1.65"

Pickup Values: Neck 6.82k, Bridge 6.91k

From the web:

  • Construction: Solid maple body wings with a full-length, neck-through-body maple design featuring a prominent "Skunk Stripe".
  • Fingerboard: Unpainted Caribbean rosewood fretboard with 20 frets and deluxe triangular pearloid inlays.
  • Electronics: Two high-output single-coil pickups managed by a 3-way toggle switch alongside independent volume and tone controls.
  • Outputs: Dual output jacks accommodating both standard mono configurations and a stereo "Rick-O-Sound" setup to route individual pickups to different amplifiers.
  • Hardware: Dual truss rod system, classic heavy-duty Rickenbacker tailpiece/bridge, and Schaller Deluxe open-gear tuners

$$$ It has just been professionally set up and dialed in for optimal tone and playability. It has low action with no buzzing. The frets are in perfect condition and polished to shine like a mirror, it is truly stage or studio ready/worthy. Including New Strings,  Perfect Intonation, Truss Rod Adjustment and Low Action Read Our Feedback and Rest Assured, The Dude Abides!


🔥 Sonic Blue Thunder – 2010 Fender Japan JB62 Jazz Bass 🔥

This is the one bass players dream about. A Fender Japan JB 62 Jazz Bass from the highly regarded 2010 era, dressed in that legendary Sonic Blue finish that looks absolutely electric under lights. The deep, vibrant blue pops with incredible depth and instantly commands attention — it’s bold, classic, and dripping with vintage Fender vibe.

Equipped with a rich rosewood fingerboard on a comfortable maple neck, this Jazz Bass delivers that signature punchy growl, tight low end, and singing highs that made the J-Bass famous. The pickups are articulate and dynamic, giving you everything from smooth, rounded fingerstyle tones to aggressive slap/pop attack and smoky, mid-forward rock tones.

Condition: Excellent for its age with only minimal playwear. This bass has been very well cared for and still looks and feels much newer than its years. No major dings, no heavy scratches — just light honest marks that prove it’s been played and loved.

Playability: Professionally set up and fully dialed in for optimal tone and effortless playability. The neck is straight, the action is perfect (low but comfortable with zero buzz), the frets are in great shape, and the electronics are quiet and responsive. It plays and sounds like a premium instrument that’s ready to go straight to rehearsal or the gig.

This Japanese Jazz Bass represents the golden era of Fender Japan quality — outstanding craftsmanship, attention to detail, and that unmistakable feel and tone that players still chase today. The JB 6262 models are especially well-regarded and have become increasingly sought after.

Comes with its original Fender gig bag (

If you’ve been looking for a Sonic Blue Jazz Bass that looks killer, sounds massive, and plays like butter — this is your bass. Don’t let this beauty swim away.

Serious inquiries only. DM for detailed photos, video, or to claim this stunner before it’s gone! 🎸💙

“Sonic Blue. Serious Boom.”​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​



🔥 This guitar (or bass) has been professionally set up and dialed in for optimal tone and playability. Unless otherwise noted in the listing, It features low action with no buzzing or dead spots, perfect intonation, a brand-new set of American-made Ernie Ball strings, and a straight neck with a fully functional truss rod. Wr make sure all frets are leveled, dressed, crowned, and polished to a mirror shine. The fingerboard has been thoroughly cleaned (unfinished rosewood and ebony boards are treated with Fret Doctor, a premium all-natural oil). All electronics function flawlessly. The guitar has been deep-cleaned and detailed and arrives stage- or studio-ready. 🔥


About The Guitar Dude 

Three-time NAMM Top 100 Dealer worldwide. Specializing in premium Gibson, classic Fender guitars from all eras and the best Japanese made treasures. Known for the highest-quality consignment sales and custom layaway plans (available on select guitars and extendable beyond 45 days to fit your budget—contact us for details).  

Often copied, never duplicated. Follow us on Facebook & Instagram for beautiful guitar content. 


Consignment Services

Like what you see? Let us go to work for you. We offer the best and most fair priced consignment service on the planet! Look for me online. @TheGuitarDude


Our Guarantee  

We stand behind every guitar we sell. “Good enough” is never good enough. Every instrument is inspected, set up, and tested by multiple people immediately before shipping. We deliver the highest standard of quality and have built a reputation as a three-time NAMM Top 100 Dealer since 2009. The Dude Abides.  


Neck & Setup - (unless otherwise noted)

- Neck is perfectly straight with no truss-rod issues. 

- Intonation is spot-on.  

- Professionally set up with low action and new American-made strings.  

- All frets are dressed, leveled, crowned (if needed), and all frets on all guitars are polished to shine like new with no divots.  


Return Policy, Restocking Fees & No Tire-Kicking Policy 

We follow Reverb’s 7-day return policy. However:  

- You must notify us in writing within 3 days of delivery (per tracking) of your intent to return.  

- Original shipping and return shipping costs are not refunded unless the guitar is damaged in transit or is significantly not as described.  

- A 15% restocking fee applies to all returns for any reason other than damage in transit or significantly not as described.  


We do our best to honestly describe and photograph every guitar and most of the noticeable imperfections. That said, we are human, if something very small can be overseen, if tiny marks, dings or super fine scratches (normal wear) bother you, please don't buy.


We record demo videos of every guitar before shipping to document exact condition and performance. Additional photos, measurements, or in-hand descriptions are available upon request prior to purchase.  


Minor adjustments and tweaks to a set up (for example to correct a slight buzz or truss-rod tweak) are normal after shipping due to carrier mishandling the package, climate & atmospheric changes, especially when moving from South Florida to colder or less humid environments. These slight issues do not constitute “significantly not as described.” Guitars must be returned in the exact same condition as received/advertised or additional repair/restocking fees will apply.  


We are a small family owned and run business, not a big-chain retailer. We invest significant time, money and effort into every guitar. This is a NO TIRE-KICKING shop. There is no post purchase haggling, returns without restocking fees or partial refunds. If minor cosmetic marks, tiny imperfections, or the possibility of a post-shipping setup tweak makes you uncomfortable, please do not buy. Plenty of large retailers offer free trials and can absorb shipping costs—we do not.  


Payment, Shipping & International Duties  

- All items can be shipped internationally. Buyer is fully responsible for all import duties, taxes, and customs fees.  

- We accept PayPal, PayPal Credit, Affirm, and other major payment methods.  

- Most U.S. shipments are sent via UPS or FedEx with full insurance, tracking, and signature confirmation. All packages are professionally packed for maximum protection.  


International Shipping Note 

International shipping prices shown are for most countries. Certain countries have higher costs. We try and avoid USPS and mostly ship UPS, but USPS is the only available or reasonable carrier in certain situations. Express services (UPS, DHL, FedEx) are always available. We can’t under value merchandise as it can not be properly insured. If you would like to discuss different avenues, contact us by phone for a personalized quote.  


Thank you for considering The Guitar Dude. We look forward to earning your business and delivering a guitar that exceeds your expectations. Contact us anytime for questions, consignment inquiries, layaway details, or a personalized shipping quote.

item.title: Greco Les Paul Short Scale Bass Model EGB-850 Mint Collection - 1991 — $1,299.99
item.description:
$1,299.99 · Very Good · Northwest Vintage Guitars

Northwest Vintage Guitars presents a rare Greco Les Paul Bass model EGB-800 from the Mint Collection in 1991.  Dare we say these are better than the real thing?  Featuring a solid Mahogany body with a Maple cap and a long tenon one piece Mahogany neck with a Rosewood fretboard.  These PU-TV6 pickups are the star of the show with very expressive mids, clear highs and a warm low end.  They read 15.6k ohms at the neck, 11.24k at the bridge.  2V, 2T, and a 3 way toggle help you dial in a myriad of cool vintage tones.  And we love the original Witch Hat knobs on this model.  

The nut measures 1.53", neck depth is .87"-1.01" at the 1st and 12th frets respectively.   It;s a comfy medium to full C profile that most folks will love.  30" Scale, 10" radius, 9.52 lbs.  Freshly strung and accurately intonated with D'Adarrio EXL 45-105 nickel strings.  The neck is straight, the truss rod turns in both directions, and the relief is currently set for optimum playability.  There are a few scratches, dings, and dents as pictured.  It remains in excellent playable condition with no dead spots or unwarranted buzzing.  There is a neck crack as pictured that has been professionally repaired here in our shop and is rock solid.  The "poker chip" on the 3 way is non original.  Comes safely packed and fully insured in the padded Fender gig bag in the photos.

item.title: Fender James Jamerson 1962 Precision Bass 3-Tone Sunburst — $2,999.99
item.description:
$2,999.99 · Brand New · The Music Emporium

James Jamerson is the reason electric bass sounds the way it does. Ranked first on virtually every "greatest bassists" list ever published, he played on an estimated 95% of Motown's charting singles between 1962 and 1972 - nearly always on the same instrument: a 1962 Precision Bass he called "The Hook." Paul McCartney, Marcus Miller, John Entwistle, Geddy Lee - the list of players who point directly to Jamerson as a primary influence is as long as it is serious.

This bass is built around that specific instrument. The era-correct neck profile matches the feel of a '62 P Bass, and the custom James Jamerson pickup is voiced to capture the dark, fundamental, authoritative tone that locked in beneath every Motown track he recorded. The Heirloom Nitrocellulose Lacquer finish ages and resonates the way the original's finish did - not a cosmetic choice, but a sonic one.

If you've spent time trying to cop that sound, you already know how much of it lives in the right bass with the right pickup and the right neck in your hand. This is where that search ends.


  • Body//Body--Alder, Finish--Heirloom Nitrocellulose Lacquer, Color--3-Color Sunburst
  • Hardware//Pickup--James Jamerson '62 Split Single-Coil Precision Bass, Controls--Master Volume / Master Tone, Bridge--Pure Vintage 4-Saddle with Threaded Steel Saddles, Pickguard--4-Ply Tortoiseshell, Tuners--Pure Vintage Reverse Open-Gear, Hardware Finish--Heirloom Nickel/Chrome, Strings--La Bella Flat Wounds .052 - .110
  • Neck//Neck--Maple, Fingerboard--Slab Rosewood, Radius--7 1/4", Profile--James Jamerson 1962 "C", Nut--1.7" Bone, Frets--Vintage Tall (20), Inlays--Clay Dots, Scale Length--34"
  • Other//Case--Vintage-Style Brown (Orange Interior), Weight--9lb. 7oz

________________________________________

The Music Emporium has been a fixture of the Boston music community since 1968. We're a shop built by musicians - players, luthiers, photographers, and gear obsessives - and that shapes everything about how we do business.

Every instrument we ship is inspected and set up by our in-house guitar techs before it leaves the shop.

We're a Fender Showcase Dealer, Collings' largest dealer worldwide, and a Martin Custom Shop Expert Dealer. Our showroom is at 165 Massachusetts Ave in Lexington, MA.

Questions about any instrument are always welcome. We're happy to talk gear.

item.title: G&L L-2000 Premium USA MFD Pickups MIJ Fujigen - 2008 — $1,599.99
item.description:
$1,599.99 · Very Good · Northwest Vintage Guitars

Northwest Vintage Guitars presents a G&L L-2000 Premium Made in Japan at the Fujigen factory and featuring the USA made MFD pickups and Tri-Tone system with 3-position pickup selector, series/parallel toggle, and active/passive/treble boost modes.  It's a beast.  G&L started making the Tribute series in Japan to feed the Japan domestic market sometime in the 90's.  Tribute production eventually moved to Korea and Indonesia, but Japan production continued under the Premium label.  These are well regarded for their high quality woods and top notch craftsmanship.  It has a very resonant Solid Ash body, maple neck, and a Rosewood fretboard.  34" scale, 9.5" radius, 8.96 lbs.  Comes safely packed and fully insured in the gig bag in the photos. 

The nut measures 1.50", neck depth is .82"-.97" at the 1st and 12th frets respectively.  It's a great comfy C profile that most folks love.  Accurately intonated and set up with low spec 2mm action.  The frets are polished and level, the fretboard is clean and conditioned.   The neck is straight, the truss rod turns in both directions, and the relief is currently set for optimum playability.  There are very few scratches and dings as pictured.  It remains in excellent playable condition with no dead spots.  One replacement knob, one coil of the neck pickup was professionally rewould by Tom Brantly.

item.title: Dunable Cyclops - Charcoal Burst #25136 — $4,942.86
item.description:
$4,942.86 · Brand New · Eddie's Guitars

The Dunable Cyclops in Charcoal Burst is a striking fusion of vintage inspired character and modern performance, crafted for players who appreciate bold design and uncompromising tone. Its beautifully figured Charcoal Burst finish is applied in a satin lacquer that enhances the natural grain of the swamp ash body while allowing the wood to resonate freely. The result is an instrument that feels alive in the hands, combining eye catching aesthetics with exceptional balance and dynamic response.

A sturdy maple neck forms the foundation of the Cyclops, delivering excellent stability and a lively, articulate attack. The ebony fretboard provides a smooth and responsive playing surface that complements the instrument's fast feel, while pearl block inlays and elegant white binding create a timeless visual contrast. Nickel Jescar FW51100 frets ensure long lasting durability and precise intonation, and Luminlay side dots offer clear visibility in low light performance environments. A white nut completes the neck with reliable tuning stability and consistent string performance.

At its core, the Cyclops is equipped with a pair of Dunable Dbird pickups that capture everything from rich vintage warmth to aggressive modern punch. The straightforward control layout features a master volume, a master tone, and a dedicated mini switch for coil splitting, expanding the tonal palette with brighter, more articulate single coil inspired sounds alongside the full power and authority of the humbuckers. Whether delivering clean passages with remarkable clarity or driving high gain amplifiers with focused aggression, the Cyclops responds with impressive sensitivity and musical expression.

Premium hardware ensures dependable performance under the demands of both studio sessions and live performance. Leo Quan Badass Clover tuners provide smooth, accurate tuning, while the matching Leo Quan Badass bridge contributes exceptional sustain, resonance, and intonation. Black hardware and the classic three ply black pickguard perfectly complement the Charcoal Burst finish, giving the instrument a refined yet unmistakably commanding appearance.

Weighing just 7 pounds 1 ounce, the Dunable Cyclops offers remarkable comfort without sacrificing tonal authority. Protected by its included hard case, it is ready for years of dependable performance wherever inspiration strikes. Blending boutique craftsmanship, versatile electronics, and unmistakable style, the Dunable Cyclops is an instrument that delivers confidence, character, and exceptional musical expression in equal measure.

Other specs include:

!!!CHIP TOP LEFT CORNER OF HEADSTOCK - DENT/DING ON THE TOP SIDE OF THE BOTTOM BOUT NEAR ARM REST BEVEL: SEE PICTURES!!!

  • Swamp Ash Body
  • Maple Neck
  • Ebony Fretboard
  • Nickel Jescar FW51100 Frets
  • White Binding
  • Luminlay Side Dots
  • Pearl Block Inlays
  • Satin Lacquer Finish Type
  • White Nut
  • Leo Quan Badass Clover Tuners
  • Leo Quan Badass Bridge
  • 3 Ply Black Pickguard
  • Black Hardware
  • Dbird Pickups
  • 1 vol, 1 tone w/ Mini switch to coil split
  • Hard Case
  • 7 lbs 1 oz
  • #25136

For more information about this Dunable instrument, please contact us.

item.title: Ibanez MDM Visions Japan Custom Shop 5-String Electric Bass with Case — $5,999.99
item.description:
$5,999.99 · Brand New · Haggerty's Music

-YOU ARE VIEWING PHOTOS OF THE ACTUAL BASS BEING SOLD!

This brand new 8.6 lb Ibanez will ship insured, fully set up, and inspected from Rapid City, SD.

Buy risk free with our 14 day - full refund, less shipping, return policy. Check out our shop policies for more details.

The Ibanez IV2MDM 5-string bass represented here showcases the kind of thoughtful craftsmanship and innovation that has made Ibanez a favorite among serious players worldwide. Visually, the combination of a striking Black Limba top and matching Black Limba control cavity cover creates an upscale, boutique-inspired appearance, while the carefully sculpted MDM body shape feels perfectly balanced whether you’re practicing at home, recording in the studio, or performing on stage. Every contour is designed with comfort in mind, giving the instrument an effortless feel that encourages long playing sessions.

Its voice is equally impressive. The Delta pickups deliver exceptional clarity and definition while maintaining a powerful, full-bodied character that cuts through a mix with ease. Notes remain articulate and focused across the entire frequency spectrum, making everything from intricate fingerstyle passages to aggressive modern grooves sound precise and expressive. The onboard Sonitech preamp and Hexa Mid system provide an incredible amount of tonal flexibility, allowing players to shape everything from warm, vintage-inspired textures to punchy, contemporary sounds with remarkable ease. Whether you’re laying down foundational bass lines, exploring solo arrangements, or navigating complex progressive passages, this instrument responds with confidence and authority.

The playing experience feels every bit as refined as the tone. The roasted maple neck has a smooth, broken-in feel while offering outstanding stability in changing environments, and the cocobolo fingerboard adds richness and complexity to every note. Stainless steel frets with Custom Shop fret edge treatment create an exceptionally smooth feel across the entire neck, while the Super All Access Neck Joint makes upper-register playing feel completely unrestricted. Combined with lightweight Gotoh hardware, the highly resonant mono-rail bridge, and the included detachable finger ramp, this is a bass designed for players who demand comfort, versatility, and uncompromising performance. Crafted in Japan and built to inspire, it delivers the premium feel and musical flexibility that discerning bassists expect. Available now at Haggerty’s Music.

item.title: Ernie Ball Music Man Bongo 6 HH 6-String Bass - Goldbar — $3,299
item.description:
$3,299 · Brand New · ProAudioStar

The Bongo represents everything a modern bass should be: well-balanced, comfortable, resonant, and expressive with effortless playability. The double-cutaway basswood body design is of superior construction and, along with a 24-fret neck, offers unimpeded access for high-register two-octave fretwork. The Bongo is available with two Ernie Ball Music Man proprietary humbucking pickups, a 4-band EQ, Neodymium magnets, and an 18V active preamp that delivers a powerful combination of unparalleled tonal diversity.

Powerful preamp and neodymium pickups

24 frets and custom half moon inlays

Ergonomic light weight tuners

Designed To Perfection

The Bongo’s uniquely shaped body design is built for comfort and provides unparalleled access to all 24 high profile frets. The maple neck is outfitted with either rosewood, ebony, or maple fretboard, half-moon inlays, and a custom neck carve, providing an ultra-comfortable playing experience.

Custom Designed Pickups

Ernie Ball Music Man custom-designed humbucking pickups feature powerful neodymium magnets, bringing you more output and a wider range of sounds.

Lightweight Hardware

Lightweight tuning machines create a more balanced neck while providing ultra-reliable tuning stability.

18-Volt Preamp

An 18-volt 4-band preamp with extra clean headroom lets you dial in everything from modern punch to classic, vintage tones.

Finest Materials

Hand-selected body and neck woods are sustainably sourced from our network of hardwood suppliers worldwide. Basswood, ebony, and maple tonewoods are individually chosen and matched for their beautiful appearance and rich tonal qualities.

item.title: Spector Banton 5 w/ Original gig bag — $1,599.99
item.description:
$1,599.99 · Excellent · The Guitar Infirmary

Here is a cool and different style of bass. Comes with original Spector gig bag. The bass is as close to "brand new" a used instrument can be. If you have any questions please feel free to reach out and I will get back to you ASAP.

Jeff

Medium-scale bass designed for punch and versatility.Continuing a 45-year legacy of innovation and top-notch craftsmanship, the Spector Bantam 5 is a medium-scale powerhouse that packs a serious punch. Like it’s 4-string counterpart, the Bantam 5 provides players with a shorter-scale instrument that doesn't sacrifice on build quality, playability or tone. With a 32” scale length and a carefully chambered body, the Bantam 5 is lightweight and extremely comfortable to play. Bantam 5 basses feature the classic NS body style, which is fully carved and contoured to the player’s body using chambered European Alder and a gorgeous figured Maple top. The deep-inset bolt-on neck ensures the utmost resonance and projection, while providing an effortless playing experience with a perfectly carved neck profile and rosewood fingerboard. The Bantam 5 is loaded with two EMG 40DC pickups, as well as an EMG BTC active EQ circuit, allowing for maximum versatility when paired with a range of pedals and amps.Features

  • Alder body
  • Bolt-on maple neck
  • Rosewood fingerboard
  • Dual EMG 40DC humbucking pickups
  • EMG BTC active EQ circuit

item.title: Yamaha Super Edition MBIII "Ladybug" Motion Bass Medium Scale 32.5" Pearl White - 1987 — $599.99
item.description:
$599.99 · Very Good · Northwest Vintage Guitars

Northwest Vintage Guitars presents a super clean Yamaha medium scale Motion Bass model MB-III with the Yamaha proprietary passive pickups and a cool Pearl White finish.  32.5" scale, 9.5" radius, 7.76 lbs.  This model features and Alder body, Maple neck, and a Rosewood fretboard.  It plays and sounds great.  Comes safely packed in the gig bag in the photos.  These are commonly referred to as the "Ladybug" bass due to the shape of their headstock.  

The nut measures 1.47", neck depth is .87"-.95" at the 1st and 12th frets respectively.  It's a medium C profile that feels great.  Freshly strung and accurately intonated with American made 45-105 nickel strings and factory spec action 2.1mm-1.75mm.  The frets are level, clean, and polished and the fretboard is clean and conditioned.  The neck is straight, the truss rod turns in both directions, and the relief is currently set for optimum playability.  It remains in excellent playable condition with no dead spots. 

We only consider local trades in person.  Most questions can be answered with a quick read of our info page.  This item is offered on our website and other online marketing platforms and subject to prior sale.  Please check our website for additional photos.

Buy with confidence. We offer a 7 day money-back return on all used guitars - all our guitars are guaranteed and a full refund will be issued if not received in the condition it was described and pictured.  We do allow “buy & try” preference returns and the buyer pays shipping charges.  Please read our shop policies for more information.   All our guitars are deep cleaned and have received a professional service and setup - an average of a $200 value. You will not get a filthy guitar from us.  If you are particular about cosmetic appearance, please message us for more details.  Additionally, all our guitars are shipped fully insured, and we will assist with any claims that may arise.  See the details on our Storefront.

Please ensure your address and phone number on this platform is the correct shipping address and contact info before purchasing.  We cannot change addresses, phones, or names after purchase.  Some shipping charges may be due before shipping if the actual shipping is more than advertised.  Platform rules prohibit dealing around the platform through messages so please don’t use messages on this platform to ask us to contact you outside of the platform.     

We are located in the Pacific Northwest USA and are exclusively online.

Fender Player II Jazz Bass, Maple Fingerboard - Cactus Gray

The Fender Player II Jazz Bass in Cactus Gray delivers iconic Fender bass tone and timeless offset style with modern updates made for todays players. Its alder body, gloss polyester finish, and classic Jazz Bass shape provide the familiar look, feel, and punch that have made the model a stage and studio favorite.

A Modern C maple neck with satin back finish, rolled-edge maple fingerboard, and 20 medium jumbo frets offers smooth, fast playability. Player Series Alnico 5 single-coil Jazz Bass pickups deliver sweet highs, growling mids, and strong lows, while the 4-saddle bridge, open-gear tuners, and flexible volume-volume-tone control layout make it easy to dial in your sound.

item.title: Ibanez EHB1506MS-BIF Bass Workshop - Black Ice Flat — $1,799.99
item.description:
$1,799.99 · B-Stock · GuitarSloth

Ibanez EHB1506MS – Black Ice Flat – Headless Multi-Scale 6-String Bass

If you’re looking to step into a high-performance 6-string multi-scale bass without breaking the bank, the Ibanez Bass Workshop EHB1506MS delivers serious value. With its modern headless design, premium components, and ultra-ergonomic feel, this bass is built for players who demand comfort, clarity, and versatility.

The lightweight African mahogany body is topped with striking poplar burl, offering a balanced tone with excellent resonance and visual appeal. Its 9-piece panga panga/walnut neck is reinforced with graphite rods for added stability, while the bound panga panga multi-scale fingerboard with stainless steel frets ensures smooth playability and long-term durability.

Equipped with Nordstrand Big Split pickups, the EHB1506MS produces fat, punchy lows with clarity and articulation. These hum-canceling pickups respond dynamically to your playing, delivering everything from aggressive growl to clean, detailed tones. A Vari-mid 3-band EQ with EQ bypass (passive tone control) gives you complete control over your sound.

The MR5HS mono-rail bridge enhances sustain and note definition by isolating each string, reducing unwanted vibration and improving clarity. Additional premium features like Schaller S-Locks, luminescent side dots, and a removable finger ramp make this bass stage- and studio-ready.

Key Features:

  • Headless, multi-scale 6-string design for modern performance
  • African mahogany body with poplar burl top
  • 9-piece panga panga/walnut neck with graphite reinforcement
  • Bound panga panga fingerboard with stainless steel frets
  • Nordstrand Big Split pickups for punchy, articulate tone
  • Vari-mid 3-band EQ with EQ bypass (passive mode)
  • MR5HS mono-rail bridge for enhanced sustain and clarity
  • Schaller S-Locks and luminescent side dots
  • Includes finger ramp

Available now at cahousemusic.com

This item is a "B-Stock Guitar" directly from the manufacturer. It is NEW Stock that has never been owned. Pictures are of the actual guitar you will receive.

we will have our licensed tech go over the guitar and set it up for you. Ibanez states the reason for the B-Stock designation is wood chip in truss cavity, poor factory repair on top, epoxy fill, and knobs off center of rout (see pictures)

item.title: Ovation Celebrity Elite E-Acoustic Bass CEB44X-7C - Cognac Burst (CEB44X-7C-G) — $627.29
item.description:
$627.29 · Brand New · Musician Madness

Ovation Celebrity Elite Plus models are special in every way.

The literal plus are the tops, which are made of visually appealing woods such as spalted/flamed/quilted maple or koa.

Furthermore, they are equipped with the Ovation-typical Multi Soundholes. This innovative construction uses several small openings at the edge of the guitar top instead of a single, central soundhole.

This has two positive effects: firstly, the available vibration

The Celebrity series is the entry into the world of Ovation. Here everyone can find their Roundback® original at an attractive price.

The acoustic sound alone is impressive, as the instruments in this line mainly feature solid spruce or cedar tops. For optimal sound development, a Scalloped Quartersawn X-Bracing - a crosswise arranged wooden construction to stabilize the top - is used on most models. The exception is the Elite Deluxe model, which is equipped with Quintad bracing (ray-shaped bars).

All guitars in the Ovation Celebrity series have a Nato neck and, depending on the model, are available in Mid-Depth, Super Shallow or Deep Contour Lyrachord® body versions.

The electro-acoustic signal processing starts with an Ovation Slimline pickup at the bridge of the guitar, which (as the name already says) stands out due to its slim design. The pressure point on the piezo crystals is increased by the serrated bridge inlay, which favours a particularly even sound. Before the signal leaves the guitar, it is refined by an Ovation OP4BT preamp with 3-band EQ and built-in tuner.

The instruments of the Celebrity series are divided into the Traditional and Elite lines, which are distinguished by the types of soundholes used (centre soundhole or multi soundholes). The additional Plus identifies guitars whose tops have been upgraded with special woods.

To round off the tonal quality, all Celebrity guitars are factory-fitted with high-quality strings from Adamas or Hannabach, depending on the model.

surface in the centre of the soundboard is increased, and secondly, the sound comes out much closer to the ear of the person playing.

Multi Soundholes, in combination with the special top woods, are visually enriching and are a guaranteed eye-catcher on stage.

  • Multi Soundhole
  • Mid-depth Cutaway Lyrachord® Body
  • Nato neck, ovangkol fingerboard and bridge
  • Scale 864 mm (34"), nut width 42,8 mm (1 11/16")
  • Perloid-Dot-Inlays

item.title: 2026 Warrior Isla J 5-String Bass Active 2026 NAMM ~ Ocean ~ J.D. Lewis's Very Last Build Before His Recent Passing — $9,999
item.description:
$9,999 · Brand New · G-Brat's Guitars

~ THIS IS THE VERY LAST BASS BUILT BY J.D. BEFORE HIS RECENT PASSING ~


Every great craftsman leaves behind a legacy. Very few have the opportunity to know which instrument will be the final chapter of that story.

This bass is that instrument.

Personally built by the legendary J.D. Lewis for the 2026 NAMM Show, this Warrior Isla J represents the final instrument to leave his workbench. Sadly, J.D. was unable to attend the show due to illness, and not long afterward, he went home to be with the Lord. His sons, Dran and Gabriel, proudly represented Warrior at NAMM and have since continued the incredible legacy their father built.

J.D. wasn't just one of the world's finest luthiers—he was a dear friend, a brother in Christ, and a man whose character was every bit as remarkable as his craftsmanship. Offering this bass for sale is both an honor and a difficult decision.

This Warrior is brand new, carries the full factory warranty, and is in absolutely flawless condition. More importantly, it represents a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to own a truly historic piece of Warrior Instruments.

Specs:

  • Model: Warrior Isla J 5-String – Built for the 2026 NAMM Show
  • Finish: Ocean, Gloss
  • Top Wood: Burled Maple, Carved
  • Back Wood: Swamp Ash, Contoured, with Natural Gloss Back Finish
  • Neck: Maple, Bolt-On, Satin Neck Finish
  • Fingerboard: Flamed Maple
  • Inlays: Extra Colorful Abalone Trapezoids with Oval Warrior Sword Inlay at the 12th Fret
  • Headstock: Burled Maple with Matching Ocean Gloss Finish and Mother-of-Pearl Warrior Logo
  • Pickups: Delano (Bridge & Neck)
  • Active Pre-Amp: Aguilar
  • Controls: Volume, Blend, Mid, Bass/Treble Stack, Passive/Active Mini Switch, Push/Pull Boost
  • Knobs: Natural Maple Wood Knobs with Matching Ocean Finish Caps
  • Hardware: Chrome
  • Tuners: Hipshot Ultralite, Open Gear
  • Scale Length: 34"
  • Bridge: Hipshot Individual Saddles, the B & E Strings are Strung through the Body
  • String Spacing at Bridge: 18.5 mm
  • Strap-Locks: Recessed
  • Input Jack: Neutrik Locking Side Jack
  • Other: Flamed Maple Back-Plate with Etched “American Handmade” Eagle
  • Weight: 8.8 lbs.
  • Serial Number: N6-26

Condition:

This bass is brand new, has never been owned by a retail customer, and remains in flawless condition. It includes the original Warrior premium padded soft case, Warrior Certificate, and the full factory warranty.

Whether you're a player, collector, or lifelong Warrior enthusiast, instruments like this simply don't surface very often. It stands as both the final creation of an extraordinary builder and the beginning of the next chapter for Warrior Instruments through Dran and Gabriel Lewis.

Some basses inspire music... This one also preserves a legacy.

Whoever brings this baby home is going to be completely STOKED!


LET'S GO!!


WHILE YOU ARE HERE, GO AHEAD AND LOOK AT MY OTHER LISTINGS!!


Me- I have a 100% positive feedback rating, and intend to keep it that way! So please don't worry! You're in good hands here!!


Shipping- We take pride in packing our guitars well, and shipping them quickly. Here are the shipping rates, covering the planet earth:

We are an authorized dealer for: Knaggs, Ken Smith, F Bass, Trussart, Warwick, Breedlove and Warrior, so for these instruments, if listed as NEW, there is a full factory warranty. For all other brands, or if listed as USED, there is no warranty available.


Return Policy- 

We do accept returns up to 14 days from the point of the initial sale, which means that the guitar must be back in our possession within this 14 day period. However, please be aware of the following conditions: If for any reason you wish to return the guitar, just call or email me directly, within 24 hours of your receiving the item, and let me know that you want to return it. Once we receive the guitar back, in the same condition, you will receive a refund, less a $50 restock fee. Also be aware that the initial shipping fee is not refundable under any circumstances.


International Buyers - We, at G-Brat's Guitars, are HONORED to do business with anybody on the planet earth... So international buyers are VERY welcome here!

The Classic Vibe ‘70s Jazz Bass® is a nod to the 1970s evolution of the J Bass®, combining the luxurious playability that made it famous with the versatility and massive tone of its dual Fender-Designed alnico single-coil pickups. Player-friendly features include a slim, comfortable “C”-shaped neck profile with an easy-playing 9.5”-radius fingerboard and narrow-tall frets, as well as a vintage-style bridge with slotted barrel saddles for solid string stability. This throwback Squier model also features 1970s-inspired headstock markings, rich-looking nickel-plated hardware and a slick vintage-tint gloss neck finish for an old-school vibe.

 Why To Buy From Rock City!

Established in 2015, Rock City Music Company is an independently owned music store for players by players. Located just outside of Detroit, Michigan, Rock City Music Company is a music mecca that encompasses everything from guitars and lessons, to vinyl records and t-shirts, to posters, accessories, our music-themed pinball machines and more.

Rock City has earned its rank  as one of the top places in the country for in-store events. Rock City has hosted legends such as Geddy Lee (Rush), Glenn Hughes (Deep Purple), Gary Holt (Slayer/Exodus), Blackberry Smoke, Nita Strauss (Alice Cooper) and many many more. Rock ’n’ Roll is our passion, and we strive to provide excellent service, and a great experience, both inside and outside of our store.

Ready to Rock, Right Out Of The Box: All of our guitars are maintained by our FMIC (Fender Musical Instruments Company) Certified in-house guitar technician, to ensure any guitar that leaves our hands is ready to play as soon as you receive it!

Fast Shipping & Communication: Our top priority is making sure that your gear arrives safely to you as soon as possible. Our staff members strive to pack and label your items for shipping within 2 hours of placing your order (During regular business hours). Items never take more than 24 hours to leave our possession.

Guitars, Basses, Amps, Cases and select other items ship via UPS Ground

Pedals & Accessories ship via USPS Priority & First Class Mail

Have a preferred shipping method? Let us know and we will do our best to accommodate

We appreciate your business more than you know and we show it! Our communication is second to none and very important to us.  You will have a dedicated and educated staff member overseeing your order, that can assist with any questions or concerns.

Is My New Guitar Protected During Shipment ? : All items are packed with extreme care and although we do our best to protect items from damage, sometimes it happens. Have no fear, all of our items are shipped fully insured at no additional cost to you.

Rock City Music Company is an official dealer of:

Reverend, Ibanez, Taylor, Martin, PRS, Gretsch, Fender, Charvel, Jackson, Epiphone, ESP/LTD, EVH, Takamine, Rock N Roll Relics, Sterling/MusicMan and Echopark Guitars.

Electro-Harmonix, Earthquaker Devices, Boss, MXR, Dunlop, Pro-Tone, Strymon and Catalinbread Effects Pedals

The American Professional II Jazz Bass® draws from more than sixty years of innovation, inspiration and evolution to meet the demands of today’s working player.

The American Pro II Jazz Bass delivers instant familiarity and sonic versatility you’ll feel and hear right away, with broad ranging improvements that add up to nothing less than a new standard for professional instruments.
